skill.name: paper-context-resolver
skill.description: Optional narrow helper skill for README-first AI repo reproduction. Use only when the README and repository files leave a narrow reproduction-critical gap and the task is to resolve a specific paper detail such as dataset split, preprocessing, evaluation protocol, checkpoint mapping, or runtime assumption from primary paper sources while recording conflicts.
skill.summary: Resolves narrow reproduction-critical gaps from research papers without overriding README guidance by default.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: All 3 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill consists only of YAML configuration and markdown documentation files with no executable code. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns are text matches on non-cryptographic content. The 'external_commands' pattern is a documentation reference to a markdown file, not shell execution. No security risks detected.

content.user_title: Resolve Paper Reproduction Gaps with Context
content.value_statement: When reproducing AI research, repository READMEs often leave critical gaps about dataset splits, evaluation protocols, or preprocessing details. This skill resolves those narrow reproduction questions from primary paper sources while preserving README-first guidance.
actual_capabilities: Resolves narrow reproduction-critical questions about dataset versions, splits, or preprocessing from paper sources
Clarifies evaluation protocol details when README documentation is incomplete
Maps checkpoint or model variant specifications from primary sources
Documents conflicts between README and paper with clear source attribution
Distinguishes between direct evidence and inference in answers
Locates primary paper sources via arXiv, OpenReview, or official project pages
limitations: Does not provide full paper summaries - only reproduction-critical details
Cannot execute code, install dependencies, or set up environments
Requires a concrete reproduction question - not for title-only paper lookups
Defers to README guidance by default and only supplements when gaps exist

use_cases.title: Clarify Dataset Split Ambiguity
use_cases.description: README mentions 'standard split' but does not specify which version. Skill identifies the exact split from the paper.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineer reproducing a vision model

use_cases.title: Resolve Evaluation Protocol Gaps
use_cases.description: README lists metrics but not evaluation settings. Skill extracts protocol details from paper methods section.

use_cases.title: Map Checkpoint Variants
use_cases.description: Repository has multiple model checkpoints. Skill identifies which matches paper's main results.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Ask About a Specific Gap
prompt_templates.prompt: The README mentions {dataset} but does not specify the train/test split. Check the paper and tell me the exact split used in experiments.
prompt_templates.scenario: You found an ambiguous dataset reference in the README

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Resolve Protocol Ambiguity
prompt_templates.prompt: The README says to use {metric} for evaluation but does not specify settings. Extract the evaluation protocol from the paper including any preprocessing, postprocessing, or threshold settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluation details are incomplete in documentation

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Document README-Paper Conflict
prompt_templates.prompt: The README instructs {x} but the paper describes {y}. Identify this conflict, explain both sources, and recommend which to follow for reproduction with justification.
prompt_templates.scenario: README and paper appear to disagree on a key detail

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Multi-Question Resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to reproduce Table 3 results. Resolve these gaps: (1) Which checkpoint variant corresponds to 'Ours' in the table? (2) What preprocessing was applied to inputs? (3) What evaluation settings were used? Cite specific sections.

output_examples.input: README says 'ImageNet validation set' but does not specify preprocessing. What preprocessing does the paper use?
output: Preprocessing: Center crop to 224x224, then normalize with mean=[0.485, 0.456, 0.406] and std=[0.229, 0.224, 0.225]
Note: This matches standard ImageNet normalization - use torchvision.transforms.Normalize with these values

output_examples.input: What train/val/test split was used for Cityscapes experiments in Table 2?
output: Split: 2975 training images, 500 validation images, 500 test images
Note: Paper uses fine annotations only; coarser annotations are excluded. README does not mention this - potential gap if using full dataset.

best_practices: Always check the README first - only invoke this skill when a concrete gap exists
Record conflicts explicitly when README and paper disagree, do not silently override
Cite specific paper sections and page numbers to enable verification
anti_patterns: Using this skill to get a full paper summary instead of resolving specific gaps
Replacing README instructions with paper guidance without documenting the conflict
Invoking with only a paper title and no concrete reproduction question

faq.question: When should I use this skill versus reading the paper myself?
faq.answer: Use this skill when you have a specific reproduction question and need to quickly locate the answer in the paper. It saves time on targeted queries but does not replace deep paper understanding.

faq.question: What if the README and paper disagree on a key detail?
faq.answer: The skill will document the conflict explicitly, explain what each source says, and preserve README-first policy. You decide which to follow with full context.

faq.question: Can this skill help me set up the environment or run commands?
faq.answer: No. This skill only resolves documentation gaps from paper sources. Use the main orchestration skill for environment setup and command execution.

faq.question: What sources does the skill search for paper information?
faq.answer: Priority order: (1) paper link from README, (2) official project/paper page, (3) arXiv or OpenReview record, (4) Google Scholar to locate primary sources.

faq.question: Does this skill work for any research paper?
faq.answer: It works best for AI/ML papers with code repositories. The focus is on reproduction-critical details like datasets, preprocessing, evaluation protocols, and checkpoint mappings.

faq.question: What happens if the paper itself is ambiguous?
faq.answer: The skill will report the ambiguity, cite relevant sections, and note when inference is required. It distinguishes between direct evidence and interpretation.
